title: amauropelta neglecta
parts: Amauropelta neglecta has many medicinal parts, such as the roots, leaves, and flowers, which have been used in traditional medicine for their therapeutic properties. The roots of Amauropelta neglecta are rich in sesquiterpenes and triterpenoids, which have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The leaves of the plant are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The flowers of Amauropelta neglecta contain essential oils, including geraniol and linalool, which have been found to have antiseptic and anti-anxiety effects.
side_effects: Amauropelta neglecta can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications. In some cases, excessive consumption of the plant may lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Additionally, individuals with certain health conditions, such as kidney or liver disease, may experience worsened symptoms or increased risk of complications if they use the plant without proper medical supervision. Improper use of Amauropelta neglecta can also cause an increased heart rate and blood pressure in some individuals.
